nameMeanings.name: Kelsey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from North or South Kelsey in Lincolnshire, so named from an uncertain initial element (perhaps an Old English personal name with genitive -es) + Old English ēg ‘island’.
Possibly also an Americanized form of German Gelzer.
History: William Kelsey was one of the founders of Hartford, CT, in 1635 (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker).

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: Roger Kelsey was born in 1520, in Tunbridge Wells, Kent, England as the son of Robert Kelsey and Elsabeth Shotte. He married Elizabeth Sutton about 1544, in Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Roger Kelsey est né en 1520, à Tunbridge Wells, Kent, Angleterre, fils de Robert Kelsey et Elsabeth Shotte. Il a épousé Elizabeth Sutton environ 1544, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Roger Kelsey wurde 1520 in Tunbridge Wells, Kent, England, als Sohn von Robert Kelsey und Elsabeth Shotte geboren. Er hat um 1544 in Kent, England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Sutton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Roger Kelsey è nato il nel 1520 nel Tunbridge Wells, Kent, England come figlio di Robert Kelsey e Elsabeth Shotte. Lui sposato Elizabeth Sutton circa 1544, nel Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie.

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Kelsey(은)는 1520년 영국 켄트 툰브리지 웰스에서 Robert Kelsey(와)과 Elsabeth Shotte의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1544년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 Elizabeth Sutton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Roger Kelsey родился в 1520 г. в местности тунбридж веллс, кэнт, Англия. Его родителей звали Robert Kelsey и Elsabeth Shotte. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Sutton около 1544 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери.
